summary: Large texpdag.c decompilation PR that replaced several placeholder asm stubs with C for HSD texture-expression DAG construction and simplification logic. The main additions were HSD_TExpMakeDag, SimplifySrc, SimplifyThis, and SimplifyByMerge, plus small matching-oriented fixes in order_dag and make_full_dependancy_mtx. The decomp.dev bot reported one new full match for make_full_dependancy_mtx and partial progress on four previously unmatched texpdag items, but also a tiny HSD_TExpSchedule regression.

key_files.role: Only changed file; gained 1011 lines and removed 12, replacing texpdag placeholders and adjusting nearby matching-sensitive code.

what_changed: Included baselib/debug.h so the new C implementations could use HSD_ASSERT.

Implemented HSD_TExpMakeDag, which walks TEV color and alpha inputs, collects unique child HSD_TExp nodes, computes distances with CalcDistance, sorts expressions by distance, and fills HSD_TExpDag entries including idx, tev pointer, depend list, nb_dep, and nb_ref.
Changed HSD_TEXP_MAX_NUM into a static int initialized to 20 for use by HSD_TExpMakeDag bounds assertions.

Implemented SimplifySrc, recursively simplifying referenced TEV sources, replacing disabled sources with a canonical cleared HSD_TEArg pattern, forwarding simple GX_TEV_ADD pass-through sources, and preserving/refcounting texture and raster resource state where allowed.
Added static data symbols HSD_TExpDag_804D5FF8 and HSD_TExpDag_804D5FFC used as the cleared HSD_TEArg bit pattern and null expression pointer.
Added helper macros CLEAR_ARG, BIAS_TO_INT, INT_TO_BIAS, and RESOURCE_CONFLICT for simplification code.

Implemented SimplifyThis, which repeatedly canonicalizes a single TEV expression by finding texture/raster argument usage, clearing unused tex/chan resources, simplifying color and alpha operations, and removing zero-input or redundant arguments.
Implemented SimplifyByMerge, which repeatedly merges compatible child TEV expressions into a parent when operations, bias, clamp, resource usage, and argument selectors allow it.

Adjusted order_dag local types from signed int to u32 for bitset variables new_available, blocked, and dep_bits, and introduced temporary pointer locals sp8, spC, and sp10 before recursive calls to improve matching.
Adjusted make_full_dependancy_mtx variable ordering/lifetime by separating flag, old, and bits handling; bot reported this brought make_full_dependancy_mtx to 100%.
smart_moves: Used explicit loop labels, pointer-walking, and manual loop counters in HSD_TExpMakeDag, matching the style often needed for older CodeWarrior output rather than over-structuring the traversal.
Modeled TEV color and alpha inputs as parallel passes instead of trying to generalize them too early; this preserved distinct c_in/a_in behavior and helped with matching-sensitive control flow.
Introduced CLEAR_ARG and the HSD_TExpDag_804D5FF8/HSD_TExpDag_804D5FFC static data pattern to centralize the exact argument-clearing idiom used throughout simplification.
Kept resource conflict checks explicit through RESOURCE_CONFLICT so merge simplification only proceeds when texture object and channel constraints are compatible.
Used ref/unref calls when replacing TEV references, showing attention to the source graph ownership semantics rather than only byte matching.

Made small type and temporary-variable changes around bitsets and recursive calls in existing functions, a common tactic when the algorithm is already known but register allocation or stack placement is blocking a match.
decomp_lessons: For DAG-building code, matching may require preserving array-backed worklists and manual pointer increments instead of cleaner index-only loops.
When traversing HSD_TExp TEV arguments, color and alpha arrays may need nearly duplicated loops because the original compiler emitted separate code paths.
Bitset variables used for dependency sets should be unsigned u32 when they feed shifts and masks; signed int can perturb comparisons and register allocation.
Static data used only as a canonical packed struct value can be represented as named local-to-file symbols when that helps recover .sdata and repeated stores.
Texture/raster simplification cannot blindly inline child expressions; tex object, tex swap, channel, and ras swap compatibility must be checked before forwarding or merging.
Refcount updates are part of the semantic match for TExp rewrites: replacement of a TEV input should pair HSD_TExpRef on the new expression with HSD_TExpUnref on the removed one when applicable.
Decomp progress can still merge with partial matches if it substantially improves large unmatched functions, but bot output should be checked for small regressions in already matched functions.
naming_conventions: Existing public-style symbols kept HSD_TExp camelcase names: HSD_TExpMakeDag, HSD_TExpSchedule, HSD_TExpSimplify, HSD_TExpRef, HSD_TExpUnref.
File-local recovered data symbols used address-style names: HSD_TExpDag_804D5FF8 and HSD_TExpDag_804D5FFC.
Matching temporaries used stack-offset-style names such as sp8, spC, sp10 and loop_count variants where semantic names were not yet clear or not matching-friendly.
Helper macros were introduced in all-caps with action/conversion names: CLEAR_ARG, BIAS_TO_INT, INT_TO_BIAS, RESOURCE_CONFLICT.
The diff preserves the misspelled existing function name make_full_dependancy_mtx rather than correcting it to dependency, avoiding ABI/symbol churn.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used goto labels such as loop_22, loop_8, loop_16, loop_36, loop_65, loop_41, loop_43, loop_54, and loop_56 to shape control flow close to the target assembly.
Added redundant-looking temporary pointer aliases before recursive order_dag calls, likely to influence stack/register allocation for order, min, and min_order.
Changed dependency bit variables to u32 to better match mask operations involving 1 << i and bitwise complements.
Manually unrolled part of the distance initialization in HSD_TExpMakeDag in groups of eight before handling the tail loop.
Used pointer arithmetic cast through u8* plus sizeof(HSD_TEArg) while iterating c_in and a_in slots, indicating a layout-driven matching tactic.
Separated flag/old/bits locals in make_full_dependancy_mtx, which decomp.dev reported as turning that function into a full match.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported 1 new full match: main/sysdolphin/baselib/texpdag make_full_dependancy_mtx improved from 98.83% to 100.00%.
Reported 5 improvements in unmatched items: SimplifyByMerge to 74.48%, SimplifyThis to 79.76%, SimplifySrc to 95.47%, HSD_TExpMakeDag to 62.16%, and .sdata to 75.00%.
Reported one tiny broken match: HSD_TExpSchedule dropped from 100.00% to 99.98% by 1 byte.
No human review comments or PR body were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching src/sysdolphin/baselib/texpdag.c to see whether HSD_TExpMakeDag, SimplifyThis, or SimplifyByMerge reached full match.
Search for fixes to the 1-byte HSD_TExpSchedule regression introduced or reported around PR 2468.
Search for later renaming or cleanup of HSD_TExpDag_804D5FF8 and HSD_TExpDag_804D5FFC once their true data meaning was understood.
Compare other HSD_TExp simplification functions for reuse of CLEAR_ARG, BIAS_TO_INT, INT_TO_BIAS, and RESOURCE_CONFLICT patterns.
